1PY6xIDkIj4.0: something's gone missing. Now, this week we're going to talk about modals with a
1PY6xIDkIj4.1: past meaning and as usual we're going to do the whole thing in 90 seconds.
1PY6xIDkIj4.2: Excuse me. So, as you know, English has modal verbs. There are nine of them in
1PY6xIDkIj4.3: total. Examples are must, can't, may, might and could. Now these verbs are normally
1PY6xIDkIj4.4: followed by the infinitive and they're special because they don't change their
1PY6xIDkIj4.5: form for person or to show time. They have a present meaning. But, when we want
1PY6xIDkIj4.6: to use them with a past meaning, we use the modal verb plus have plus the past
1PY6xIDkIj4.7: participle. In this case, Rob must have eaten my cake. Now, Rob must have eaten my
1PY6xIDkIj4.8: cake is a measure of confidence. It means that I am extremely confident that Rob
1PY6xIDkIj4.9: did this at some time in the past. When we change the modal verb, we change the
1PY6xIDkIj4.10: level of confidence. For example, Rob can't have eaten my cake. This means I
1PY6xIDkIj4.11: think that this is impossible but I know he did it.

2tM1LFFxeKg.2: Your brain and muscles are perfectly poised to help you achieve this goal.
2tM1LFFxeKg.3: First, your brain sends a signal to motor neurons inside your arm.
2tM1LFFxeKg.4: When they receive this message, they fire,
2tM1LFFxeKg.5: causing muscles to contract and relax,
2tM1LFFxeKg.6: which pull on the bones in your arm and generate the needed movement.
2tM1LFFxeKg.7: The bigger the challenge becomes, the bigger the brain's signal grows,
2tM1LFFxeKg.8: and the more motor units it rallies to help you achieve your task.
2tM1LFFxeKg.9: But what if the door is made of solid iron?
2tM1LFFxeKg.10: At this point, your arm muscles alone won't be able to generate
2tM1LFFxeKg.11: enough tension to pull it open,
2tM1LFFxeKg.12: so your brain appeals to other muscles for help.
2tM1LFFxeKg.13: You plant your feet, tighten your belly, and tense your back,
2tM1LFFxeKg.14: generating enough force to yank it open.
2tM1LFFxeKg.15: Your nervous system has just leveraged the resources you already have,
2tM1LFFxeKg.16: as well as other muscles, to meet the demand. While all this is happening,
2tM1LFFxeKg.17: your muscle fibers undergo another complete transformation.
